target.name: Stark Defence
target.founder: Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, Johannes Schaback

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Stark Defence is a Series A defense and security company founded by Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, and Johannes Schaback, currently valued at $500M post-money on $100M+ raised. The comparable universe consists of 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector, including Scale AI, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $1.0B based on the $500M last post-money. Peer-median analysis across 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Scale AI ($29.0B), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields a median implied value of $300M, with a range from $150M to $29.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in defense tech valuations, particularly given Scale AI's substantial outlier position.

Triangulation of both methods supports a range of $255M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $650M. The current $500M valuation falls within this range but notably below the stage-progression implication. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier given market conditions and capital availability, and the relevance of the Scale AI comparable given its significantly higher valuation relative to peer peers. The peer set's wide dispersion limits confidence in the median approach alone.
